prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel plant called a jax has a property: it can be activated by one of its colored leafs. Your job is to figure out which plant in the perception scenario is the jax-analog, and therefore which colored leaf on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three plants in a garden: a jax, a scob, and a vro. The jax is-growing-under the scob. The scob is-to-the-left-of the vro. The jax has a blue colored leaf on top, a purple colored leaf on side, and a yellow colored leaf on bottom. The scob has a purple colored leaf on top and a blue colored leaf on bottom. The vro has a yellow colored leaf on top and a blue colored leaf on side. Clipping the blue leaf from the jax activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three plants in a garden: a corp, a trob, and a nop. The trob is-growing-under the nop. The nop is-to-the-left-of the corp. The corp has a yellow colored leaf on top and a purple colored leaf on bottom. The trob has a blue colored leaf on top, a purple colored leaf on side, and a yellow colored leaf on bottom. The nop has a purple colored leaf on top and a blue colored leaf on side.

Which plant in the perception scenario is the jax-analog, and which colored leaf activates it?
